query: Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool repair status latest news June July 2026, algae paint peeling, is the pool fully repaired, lawsuit status
answer:
I've completed thorough research on the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool repair status. Here's a summary of the key findings (as of July 2, 2026):

**Is the pool fully repaired? — NO.** As of the research date, repairs had not yet begun. The NPS stated in a June 25 federal court filing that it will drain and repair the pool **after** July 4 celebrations, and the contractor (Rhino Linings) said repairs will take "weeks."

**Algae & peeling paint:**
- A ~$16M Trump-ordered renovation (blue "American Flag Blue" polyurea liner + nanobubble ozone filtration) was completed in early June 2026.
- Within weeks: water turned green from an algae bloom, and the blue liner/coating began peeling (chunks floating to the surface, first observed mid-June).
- NPS treated algae with hydrogen peroxide and nanobubble ozone tech; the coating damage requires draining and larger repairs post-July 4.

**Cost:** Escalated from Trump's ~$1–2M/one-week estimate to ~$14.7M (Atlantic Industrial Coatings) + ~$1.7M (Greenwater Services) — no-bid contracts, ~$16M total.

**Vandalism claims:** Trump blamed vandals for a "350-foot gash" without evidence; NPS confirmed caulk was "cut with a sharp knife or razor" (June 9) but didn't confirm vandalism. Citation counts varied (up to 14 per U.S. Attorney Pirro).

**Lawsuit status — ONGOING:** *Cultural Landscape Foundation v. DOI* (No. 1:26-cv-01593, D.D.C.), filed May 11, 2026, alleges NHPA/NEPA violations. Judge Carl Nichols held a preliminary-injunction hearing (~May 21); the administration filed a motion to dismiss. No final injunction ruling was found in the record as of July 2.

Full synthesis, key facts, data points, and 20 sourced citations have been delivered via the structured output.

sources.title: Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
sources.snippet: In 2026 the pool was resurfaced at the order of Trump with a blue polyurea coating and a nanobubbler filtration system.

key_facts: As of July 2, 2026 the Reflecting Pool is NOT fully repaired; NPS plans to drain and repair it AFTER July 4 celebrations (per June 25 federal court filing by NPS Deputy Director Frank Lands).

Contractor Rhino Linings VP Francois Rivard said (June 22) repairs will take 'weeks' and will NOT be done before July 4; the pool remains watertight and damage is limited to the cosmetic/aesthetic layer.
The ~$16 million Trump-ordered renovation (completed early June 2026) added a blue 'American Flag Blue' polyurea liner and a nanobubble ozone filtration system.
Within weeks the water turned green from an algae bloom (experts: natural Desmodesmus bloom accelerated by the dark surface) and the blue coating/liner began peeling (observed mid-June).
NPS treated algae with hydrogen peroxide and nanobubble ozone technology; officials claimed water became 'crystal clear' by late June.

Costs: Trump first estimated ~$1-2M/one week (April), then 'less than $20M' (May 21); final ~$14.7M main coating contract (Atlantic Industrial Coatings) + ~$1.7M nanobubble (Greenwater Services); contracts were no-bid.
Sika Corp., the 2010 renovation firm, declined the job in March 2026 as 'unfeasible' given the July 4 deadline and 2.5 miles of expansion joints.

Trump blamed 'sick, deranged people'/vandals for a '350-foot gash' and fertilizer dumping, offering no evidence; NPS confirmed caulk was 'cut with a sharp knife or razor' on/before June 9 and ~70 fence posts thrown in, but did not confirm vandalism or name suspects.

Arrest/citation counts varied: Trump said 6 arrests; Interior cited 7 arrests/7 citations (later 5/5); U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro said 14 cited (June 30), facing up to 10 years. Olympic canoeist David Hearn was detained and denied wrongdoing.
Lawsuit: Cultural Landscape Foundation v. U.S. Dept. of the Interior, No. 1:26-cv-01593 (D.D.C.), filed May 11, 2026, alleging NHPA and NEPA violations; Judge Carl Nichols presiding.

Trump administration filed a motion to dismiss the lawsuit; a preliminary-injunction hearing was held ~May 21 (Judge Nichols weighed reversibility); no final injunction ruling found in the record as of July 2, 2026.
Dead ducks reported; Center for Biological Diversity sought an environmental investigation, citing Rhino 405 epoxy labeled 'toxic to aquatic life with long-lasting effects.'
Congressional Democrats (Sens. Merkley and Heinrich; Rep. Garcia) called for investigations into the no-bid contracts; Trump threatened to sue ABC News over its cost reporting.

data_points.metric: Main coating contract cost (Atlantic Industrial Coatings)
Alleged liner cut/gash length claimed by Trump
Fence post tops thrown into pool
Vandalism citations (per U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro)
Maximum potential prison sentence for defacing site
